# gdalvector

``` plaintext
              __      __                __
   ____ _____/ /___ _/ /   _____  _____/ /_____  _____
  / __ `/ __  / __ `/ / | / / _ \/ ___/ __/ __ \/ ___/
 / /_/ / /_/ / /_/ / /| |/ /  __/ /__/ /_/ /_/ / /
 \__, /\__,_/\__,_/_/ |___/\___/\___/\__/\____/_/
/____/

gdalvector v0.0.1 - Modern Package for GDAL Vector Data
```

## Overview

> \[!NOTE\] `gdalvector` is a modern-focused R package for working with
> cloud native geospatial vector data via bindings to GDAL from the
> exceptional [gdalraster](https://github.com/firelab/gdalraster)
> package. This package offers a more focused intent on the vector side
> of what the underlying GDAL bindings provide and can be thought of as
> an extension of `gdalraster` that is also orthogonal and focused on
> vector specific data operations and modern GDAL algorithmic api
> focused workflows.

The goal of `gdalvector` is to …..

## Installation

You can install the development version of `gdalvector` from GitHub with
`pak`:

``` r

pak::pak("jimbrig/gdalvector")
```

> \[!WARNING\] Note that on Windows, you should look into the
> [`gdalraster.windows`](https://github.com/jimbrig/gdalraster.windows)
> package I have created to provide the ability to work with
> `gdalraster`’s `GDALAlg` C++ binding reference class to the core GDAL
> Algorithmix C API. For these same reasons, and due to the fragility of
> the underlying C/C++ ABIs in general for GDAL, and the cross-platform
> nuances at play (especially for Windows), this package is likely not
> one that will ever be distributed on CRAN officially, or its at least
> not a goal of mine.

To get `gdalraster.windows` up and running and verify proper bindings to
the algorithmic APIs you can run:

``` r

pak::pak("jimbrig/gdalraster.windows")
gdalraster.windows::install_gdal_runtime()
gdalraster.windows::install_gdalraster()
library(gdalraster.windows)
gdalraster::gdal_global_reg_names()
```

## Examples

## Resources

# Package index

## All functions

- [`as_config_option()`](http://docs.jimbrig.com/gdalvector/reference/as_config_option.md)
  : Convert GDAL Configuration Options to a Config-Option Vector

- [`as_gdal_args()`](http://docs.jimbrig.com/gdalvector/reference/as_gdal_args.md)
  : Convert GDAL Options to Algorithm Arguments

- [`as_gdal_config_opts()`](http://docs.jimbrig.com/gdalvector/reference/as_gdal_config_opts.md)
  : Coerce to GDAL Configuration Options

- [`as_gdal_creation_opts()`](http://docs.jimbrig.com/gdalvector/reference/as_gdal_creation_opts.md)
  : Coerce to GDAL Creation Options

- [`as_gdal_open_opts()`](http://docs.jimbrig.com/gdalvector/reference/as_gdal_open_opts.md)
  : Coerce to GDAL Open Options

- [`as_gdal_vsi_opts()`](http://docs.jimbrig.com/gdalvector/reference/as_gdal_vsi_opts.md)
  : Coerce to GDAL VSI Options

- [`as_gdalg()`](http://docs.jimbrig.com/gdalvector/reference/as_gdalg.md)
  : Coerce to GDALG

- [`check_available_ram()`](http://docs.jimbrig.com/gdalvector/reference/check_available_ram.md)
  : Check Available RAM

- [`check_inherits()`](http://docs.jimbrig.com/gdalvector/reference/check_inherits.md)

  : Check Not Empty

  : GeoParquet Schema

  : GeoParquet Schema Info

  Ping

  : Remote File Download with Change Detection

  : Check if a Remote File Exists at a Given URL

  : Get the Hash of a Remote File from its URL

  Perform a `HEAD` HTTP Request for a Remote URL

  : List files at a remote HTTP directory index

- [`remote_size()`](http://docs.jimbrig.com/gdalvector/reference/remote_size.md)
  : Get the Size of a Remote File from its URL

  :

  Build a SQL `ST_IsValid` WHERE Clause

  :

  `sys_available_ram` - System Available RAM

  `sys_num_cpus` - System Number of CPUs

  `sys_os` - System OS Name

  :

  `sys_path` - System PATH

- [`sys_pid()`](http://docs.jimbrig.com/gdalvector/reference/sys_pid.md)
  :

  `sys_pid` - System Process ID

- [`sys_platform()`](http://docs.jimbrig.com/gdalvector/reference/sys_platform.md)
  :

  `sys_platform` - System Platform

- [`sys_which()`](http://docs.jimbrig.com/gdalvector/reference/sys_which.md)
  :

  `sys_which` - System `which`

- [`validate_gdal_opts()`](http://docs.jimbrig.com/gdalvector/reference/validate_gdal_opts.md)
  : Validate GDAL Options Against Driver Metadata

- [`vsi_azure()`](http://docs.jimbrig.com/gdalvector/reference/vsi_azure.md)
  :

  Build a `/vsiaz/` Path

- [`vsi_curl()`](http://docs.jimbrig.com/gdalvector/reference/vsi_curl.md)
  :

  Wrap a URL with `/vsicurl/`

- [`vsi_exists()`](http://docs.jimbrig.com/gdalvector/reference/vsi_exists.md)
  : Test Whether a VSI Path Exists

- [`vsi_from_uri()`](http://docs.jimbrig.com/gdalvector/reference/vsi_from_uri.md)
  : Convert a URI to a GDAL VSI Path

- [`vsi_handlers()`](http://docs.jimbrig.com/gdalvector/reference/vsi_handlers.md)
  : List VSI Handlers Used by a Path

- [`vsi_meta()`](http://docs.jimbrig.com/gdalvector/reference/vsi_meta.md)
  : Get VSI File Metadata

- [`vsi_path()`](http://docs.jimbrig.com/gdalvector/reference/vsi_path.md)
  : Convert a Path to a GDAL VSI Path

- [`vsi_size()`](http://docs.jimbrig.com/gdalvector/reference/vsi_size.md)
  : Get the Size of a VSI Path

- [`vsi_strip()`](http://docs.jimbrig.com/gdalvector/reference/vsi_strip.md)
  : Strip the Outermost VSI Handler

- [`vsi_sync()`](http://docs.jimbrig.com/gdalvector/reference/vsi_sync.md)
  : Sync Between VSI Paths

- [`vsi_type()`](http://docs.jimbrig.com/gdalvector/reference/vsi_type.md)
  : Get the Type of a VSI Path

- [`vsi_zip()`](http://docs.jimbrig.com/gdalvector/reference/vsi_zip.md)
  :

  Wrap a Path with `/vsizip/`

- [`vsi_zip_curl()`](http://docs.jimbrig.com/gdalvector/reference/vsi_zip_curl.md)
  :

  Build a `/vsizip//vsicurl/` Path

- [`xml_parse_gdal_driver_config_opts()`](http://docs.jimbrig.com/gdalvector/reference/xml_parse_gdal_driver_config_opts.md)
  : Parse GDAL Driver Configuration Options from XML

- [`xml_parse_gdal_options()`](http://docs.jimbrig.com/gdalvector/reference/xml_parse_gdal_options.md)
  : Parse XML for GDAL Options

### All vignettes

- [gdalvector](http://docs.jimbrig.com/gdalvector/articles/gdalvector.md):
